---
title: "Package"
---

Working notes on the R package API design and refactoring decisions. This chapter will be removed or reorganized once the refactor is merged.

## Naming Conventions

Public-facing functions use dot notation to mirror base R (e.g., `cor.test`, `cov`):

- `ece.cov` ‚Äî equivariant covariance estimator (replaces `equiv.cov`)
- `ece.cor` ‚Äî equivariant correlation estimator (corresponds to `cor`)
- `ece.complexity` ‚Äî mean heterogeneity / complexity of the series
- `ece.se` ‚Äî asymptotic standard error of the correlation estimator
- `ece.test` ‚Äî hypothesis test for correlation (corresponds to `cor.test`)

Internal helpers use snake_case (e.g., `ece_pair`, `var_corr_est`).

## Design Decisions

### `ece_pair` (internal)

The internal workhorse for pairwise computation. Returns `list(cov, complexity)` for a single pair of vectors. Not exported. Pulled out of `equiv.cov` (formerly `compute_equiv`).

### `ece.cov`

Mirrors the interface of base `cov()`: two vectors ‚Üí scalar, matrix ‚Üí p√óp matrix. Calls `ece_pair` internally.

### `ece.complexity`

Captures the slope of lagged differences as a function of lag. Large values indicate large or frequent mean shifts. Same interface as `ece.cov`.

### `ece.test`

Accepts an n√ó2 matrix only (no `Y = NULL`). Dispatches on `type`:

- `type = "z.test"` ‚Äî asymptotic normal test based on `ece.cor`
- `type = "bs.multiplier"` ‚Äî multiplier bootstrap test (planned)

Returns `list(estimate, p.value)`. `conf.int` was dropped as it was not functional.

### `var_corr_est`

Now takes scalars `(n, sx, sy, sxy, wx, wy, wxy)` directly instead of an `ece_obj`. Renamed to `ece.se` at the public level.

## Refactor Checklist

### Completed

- [x] Develop unit testing suite
- [x] `lag_diff`: inputs lowercased, `y = x` default
- [x] `ece.cov` corresponds to `cov`; helper pulled out as `ece_pair`; `ece.complexity` added as a separate function
- [x] `ece.cor` corresponds to `cor`
- [x] `var_corr_est` renamed to `ece.se`; decoupled from `ece_obj`
- [x] `ece.test`: matrix-only input, drop `conf.int`, add `type` argument

### Pending

#### 1. Immediate cleanup (finish what we started)

- [ ] Delete `equiv.cov`
- [ ] Resolve `ece_obj` design pattern ‚Äî consider renaming to `ece.fit`
- [ ] Rewrite `theory-estimators.R` ‚Äî naming is poor throughout

#### 2. Core utilities

- [ ] `lag_terms` ‚Äî new function; test that it sums to `lag_diff`; use `X - rotate(X, k = 1)`
- [ ] `mod_idx` ‚Äî poor name; only used once; consider moving or inlining
- [ ] `circ.eigendecomp` ‚Äî new feature

#### 3. Data generation layer

- [ ] `demean` should not take `params` as a parameter
- [ ] `generate_data`: `scenario13` breaks the interface; needs fixing
- [ ] Resolve inconsistency between `config` and `params`

#### 4. Structural refactors

- [ ] `utils.R` ‚Äî clean up `_vec` / `_mat` / dispatch triple pattern
- [ ] `scenario.R` ‚Äî consolidate ~30 numbered functions into a parameterized form
- [ ] `X` should be accepted as a multivariate matrix throughout

#### 5. Tests

- [ ] New unit-test file for mathematical facts
  - [ ] `lag_diff` corresponds to `lag_mat`
  - [ ] Two representations of variance
- [ ] Tests for untested functions in `utils.R` and `theory.R`

#### 6. New features and fixes

- [ ] `demean.test`
- [ ] `segment.test`: fix degrees of freedom
- [ ] `ece.test`: add `type = "bs.multiplier"`

#### 7. Simulation and documentation

- [ ] Produce master simulation file (like RCV)
